plan_adherence_notes: All 6 planned steps were implemented: (1) useAnimationPropagation hook created with BFS, staggered timeouts, cancellation (169 lines vs estimated 100), (2) SVG filter defs and CSS keyframes added, (3) PADDING increased 40→60, (4) assumption change detection wired via useEffect, (5) animation state integrated into node/edge rendering, (6) ProbabilityBar highlight prop added with box-shadow transition. However, adding useAssumptions() to BayesNetGraph broke all 13 existing BayesNetGraph tests — they now fail with 'useAssumptions must be used within AssumptionProvider'. The plan did not include a step for updating tests, and the execution did not address this regression.
missed_questions: How will adding new context dependencies (useAssumptions) to BayesNetGraph affect existing tests? — This gap directly caused 13 test failures since tests don't wrap BayesNetGraph in AssumptionProvider.
Should the animation respect prefers-reduced-motion for accessibility?
What is the performance impact of calling setState on every BFS hop (re-renders the entire graph per layer)?
reason: Implementation faithfully follows the plan across all 6 steps and the code quality is good (clean BFS, proper cleanup, interruptible animation). Research was thorough with all 7 questions answered at ≥0.85 confidence and all assumptions verified. However, the execution introduced a regression: all 13 BayesNetGraph tests now fail because the component calls useAssumptions() but tests lack an AssumptionProvider wrapper. The questions phase missed asking about test impact, which is the root cause. Changes requested: (1) fix BayesNetGraph tests by wrapping test renders in AssumptionProvider, (2) consider adding a prefers-reduced-motion check to skip animations for accessibility.
